items.title: Insurance sector faces slowdown, but Tune Protect eyes growth
items.summary: He said the insurer is targeting growth above the industry average by strengthening its general insurance business, expanding travel-related offerings and growing ancillary services. She said Tune Protect remains cautious about the travel insurance segment due to ongoing geopolitical tensions, particularly in the Middle East. Koot said Tune Protect's growth strategy focuses on three areas namely its Malaysian general insurance business, regional travel insurance operations and ancillary products and services.

items.title: Daim's daughter wins bid to recover passport
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court today ordered the return of the passport of Asnida Daim, daughter of the late former finance minister Tun Daim Zainuddin, within seven days. Therefore, to link the non-compliance issue to a risk of flight if the passport is returned to and retained by the applicant (Asnida) is unjustified. Rajesh argued that the Sessions Court imposed the passport surrender condition without supporting evidence or justification from the prosecution, in breach of established legal principles and Asnida's constitutional rights.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan DAP to keep over half of its incumbents for state polls
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 10 — Negeri Sembilan DAP will retain more than half of its incumbent representatives as candidates in the upcoming state election as it seeks to maintain its impressive record of winning every seat it has contested since 2013. He said the party also intends to field several new faces in the election as it seeks to defend the 11 seats it won in the last state election. The 2023 Negeri Sembilan state election saw Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) form the state government after securing 31 of the 36 seats.

items.title: Taiwan fires battle-tested rockets in 'shoot-and-scoot' anti-invasion drill
items.summary: TAICHUNG: Taiwan's military on Wednesday fired its new mobile HIMARS rocket system, which is widely used by Ukraine, simulating an attack on an invading Chinese force and demonstrating its ability to "shoot-and-scoot" by avoiding counter strikes. Taiwan test fired its Lockheed Martin-made High Mobility Artillery Rocket Systems, or HIMARS, for the first time last year off its east coast. Wednesday marked the first time it has fired the precision weapon on the west coast, in central Taiwan's Taichung.

items.title: Kelantan offers free bus rides to cultural festival, targets 300,000 visitors
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Kelantan is offering free bus rides from Kuala Lumpur to its annual cultural festival as the state targets 300,000 visitors, including tourists from Thailand. State Tourism, Culture, Arts and Heritage Committee chairman Datuk Kamarudin Md Nor said the initiative was aimed at encouraging more visitors, particularly those from outside the state, to attend the Kelantan People's Cultural Festival 2026. He said the festival was expected to attract about 300,000 visitors, including residents from Kelantan and neighbouring states.

items.title: Alliance Bank commits RM2bil to boost SME financing access
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Alliance Bank Malaysia Bhd has committed up to RM2 billion in financing solutions for SMEs through a partnership with Credit Guarantee Corp Malaysia Bhd (CGC). Under the arrangement, Alliance Bank will be the financing provider, while CGC will provide guarantee support to help businesses with limited collateral access funding. Alliance Bank group chief executive officer Kellee Kam said the initiative reflects the bank's commitment to supporting Malaysian SMEs.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan Bersatu says it will contest election under PN banner despite PAS split
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — Negeri Sembilan Bersatu has said it will continue to contest under the Perikatan Nasional (PN) banner in the next state election, despite PAS’ decision to end political cooperation with the party. PN currently holds five seats in the Negeri Sembilan state assembly, three through PAS and two through Bersatu. Meanwhile, Utusan Malaysia reported that Negeri Sembilan PAS commissioner Mohd Fairuz Mohd Isa reportedly said no decision had been made on whether PAS would use the PN logo or contest under the PN ticket in the next state election.

items.title: MyDigital ID's high-security features can help curb online fraud — CCID director
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The MyDigital ID system, equipped with high-security features, has the potential to curb fraud while strengthening identity verification, says Bukit Aman. Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) director Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa said police held two engagement sessions with MyDigital ID and were confident its full deployment could help prevent online scams. National Taskforce to Combat Fraud chairman Syed Ahmad Taufik Albar said integration of MyDigital ID with banks' Know Your Customer (KYC) systems would strengthen anti-fraud efforts.

items.title: Bullying cases drop at MRSMs with former military wardens
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Several Maktab Rendah Sains Mara (MRSM) schools have recorded fewer disciplinary and bullying cases since former military personnel were appointed as full-time wardens. "Mara's approach of placing former military personnel at MRSM has proven effective in addressing bullying. All 58 MRSM schools would have full-time wardens from among former uniformed personnel beginning next year, selected through a strict screening process.

items.title: Sabah's Gaya Island cleanliness push aims to attract more tourists
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Kota Kinabalu City Hall (DBKK) plans to strengthen cleanliness efforts on Gaya Island to attract more tourists. Mayor Datuk Seri Sabin Samitah said the island's natural beauty could attract more visitors if cleanliness was improved. "If we look around, this island is beautiful.

items.title: Former CFO of condom maker Karex charged with insider trading
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A former chief financial officer of condom manufacturer Karex Bhd was charged in the Sessions Court today with insider trading involving the company's shares six years ago. Goh Chok Siang, 54, was accused of acquiring 150,000 units of Karex shares on July 22, 2020, while in possession of material non-public information regarding the company's intention to venture into the manufacturing and sale of gloves. Deputy public prosecutors Ng Chian Huey and Izzat Fauzan, together with prosecuting officer Mark Rohan Mahadevan, prosecuted, while Wong Chiang Kiat appeared for the accused.

items.title: Perak closes hiking trail for up to 6 months after missing hiker incident
items.summary: KUANTAN: A hiking trail in Perak will be closed for up to six months while authorities review safety procedures following a case in which a woman hiker went missing for 14 days before being found safe. The Perak Forestry Department is reviewing standard operating procedures (SOPs) for future hiking activities at Gunung Batu Putih. "We need to review and improve several SOPs to ensure compliance among Forestry Mountain Guides (FMG) and hikers.

items.title: Malaysian banks honour 68 staff who helped save RM139m from scammers last year
items.summary: The banking personnel were feted at the second consecutive #JanganKenaScam Bank Heroes Recognition Event 2026, a nationwide anti-scam initiative organised by The Association of Banks in Malaysia (ABM) and The Association of Islamic Banking and Financial Institutions Malaysia (Aibim), in close collaboration with the police. The associations said the numbers reflect the aggressive scale of modern financial crime and the corresponding ramp-up in banking defences. National Taskforce to Combat Fraud chairman Syed Ahmad Taufik Albar speaks at the #JanganKenaScam Bank Heroes Recognition Event 2026 in Petaling Jaya.

items.title: JB Sentral closure: KTMB activates bus transfers amid RTS Link project works from June 11
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — Train services at JB Sentral Station will be temporarily suspended starting tomorrow to facilitate the launching of the truss structure for the Johor Bahru–Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link Project. Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) in a statement today said that during the closure period, passengers involved in the ETS EP9535 and EG9449 trains will continue their journey using the intermediary bus service provided between Kempas Baru Station and JB Sentral. Affected passengers can contact the KTMB Call Centre at 03-9779 1200 for further information.

items.title: Packaging rebound lifts Scientex's Q3 earnings to RM142.2mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Scientex Bhd posted a 14.8 per cent increase in net profit for the third quarter ended April 30, 2026 (Q3 FY26) to RM142.2 million from RM123.9 million a year earlier. The packaging division recorded revenue of RM630 million, up 2.5 per cent from RM614.8 million previously, supported by higher sales volume. The company's property division maintained steady performance, generating revenue of RM487.8 million compared with RM495.8 million a year ago.

items.title: DAP to retain over half of incumbents for Negri election
items.summary: He said the party also intends to field several new faces in the election as it seeks to defend the 11 seats it won in the last state election. The 2023 Negri Sembilan election saw Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) form the state government after securing 31 of the 36 seats. PH won 17 seats while BN secured 14 seats, with Perikatan Nasional winning five seats.

items.title: MyDigital ID integration could boost fraud detection
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The full integration of MyDigital ID into banks' know your customer systems could strengthen the banking sector's verification capabilities, making it a valuable tool in combating scams, industry executives said. The national taskforce to combat fraud chairman Syed Ahmad Taufik Albar said there is currently no specific timeline for the full integration of MyDigital ID. They all have their respective timelines in terms of adopting MyDigital ID and exploring more use cases on how to utilise MyDigital ID to prevent scams," said Syed Ahmad, who is also Malayan Banking Bhd group chief executive officer of community financial services.

items.title: Sabah baker injured in home accident becomes early beneficiary of Socso’s 24-hour protection scheme
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — A young man from Sabah who fell from a staircase at home has become one of the first beneficiaries under the Non-Work-Related Accident Scheme (SKBBK), or LINDUNG 24 Jam, which took effect on June 1. In a statement today, the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) said the victim, Arnold Nathanael Lister, 24, qualified for coverage as an active contributor, despite the accident happening outside working hours. Perkeso noted that previously, domestic accident victims often struggled to get financial protection, but the new scheme now covers treatment costs and safeguards the livelihoods of contributors.

items.title: How Rosmah's RM67.5mil jewellery case unfolded: A timeline
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Global Royalty claimed that it sent 44 pieces of jewellery including diamond necklaces, earrings, rings, bracelets and tiaras, each worth US$124,000 (RM519,183) to US$925,000 (RM3.8 million) to Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor through hand-carry courier via its two agents. Today, the High Court ordered Rosmah to pay RM67.5 million to the jeweller after finding her liable for failing to return 43 pieces of jewellery entrusted to her on a consignment basis. June 26, 2018 - Global Royalty filed the lawsuit against Rosmah.

items.title: In Tokyo, Anwar pitches Malaysia-Japan alliance for chips, AI and clean energy
items.summary: TOKYO, June 10 — Malaysia and Japan can make a meaningful contribution to strengthening regional supply chains, particularly in semiconductors, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. He said Malaysia’s strengths in assembly, testing and advanced packaging complement Japan’s leadership in materials, equipment and advanced manufacturing, creating opportunities to build more resilient and integrated technology ecosystems. In energy, Anwar said the partnership could support Asia’s transition to a lower-carbon future while maintaining economic growth and energy security.

items.title: 4 remanded for 7 days over alleged Perkeso false claims
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Four suspects, including a woman, have been remanded for seven days over alleged false claims involving Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) Daya Kerjaya employment incentives. Perkeso's Daya Kerjaya initiative provides training and job placement assistance to help unemployed individuals improve their skills and secure employment. Yesterday, MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said the commission had detected misappropriation involving the government initiative, with false claims amounting to RM9.01 million.

items.title: Ex-CFO of Malaysian condom maker Karex claims trial to insider trading over 150,000 shares
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — A former chief financial officer of Karex Berhad pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of acquiring 150,000 shares in the company while in possession of undisclosed inside information six years ago. According to the charge, Goh Chok Siang, 55, allegedly acquired 150,000 units of Karex Berhad shares through a Central Depository System (CDS) account while having information that was not generally available to the public. The offence was allegedly committed at Bursa Malaysia Securities Berhad, Exchange Square, Bukit Kewangan, on July 22, 2020.

items.title: PAS taps Annuar Musa to lead Johor election campaign
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 10 — PAS has appointed former federal minister Tan Sri Annuar Musa as its Johor election director ahead of the upcoming state polls. Johor PAS information chief Mohd Firdaus Jaffar said the appointment was aimed at strengthening the party’s preparations for the election. “PAS Johor welcomes Tan Sri Annuar Musa’s appointment as the party’s state election director.

items.title: Malaysia child marriage: Suhakam pushes to raise Muslim legal marriage age to 18
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam) is advocating a nationwide minimum marriage age of 18 for Muslims, while retaining a judicial mechanism that would allow minors aged 16 and 17 to apply for permission to marry. Suhakam Chief Children's Commissioner Dr Farah Nini Dusuki said the proposed reform was aimed at safeguarding children's rights, education and health, while ensuring marriage remained an option only in exceptional cases involving minors. "We are not restricting the age of marriage.

items.title: University students across South Korea to protest in ballot shortage row
items.summary: SEOUL: University students across South Korea will stage protests on Wednesday as anger grew over ballot paper shortages at recent local elections. Dozens of polling stations nationwide were short of around 7,000 ballot papers on June 3 election day, official data showed. The local election was the first nationwide vote since President Lee Jae Myung took office following conservative Yoon Suk Yeol's ouster over his brief martial law declaration in late 2024.

items.title: Five days, unlimited durian, zero apologies: Penang’s new Hong Kong tour is every fruit lover’s dream
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 10 — Penang has rolled out its first official durian-themed tourism package in Hong Kong, marking a major step forward in the state’s bid to boost its international tourism profile and promote its signature durian experiences. State Tourism and Creative Economy Committee chairman Wong Hon Wai said the initiative, a collaboration between Tourism Malaysia’s Hong Kong office, Penang Global Tourism (PGT) and Wing On Travel, aims to draw more Hong Kong tourists through a travel package built entirely around Penang’s celebrated durian industry. This collaboration represents a milestone for Penang tourism.

items.title: Warisan remains strong despite defections, says Shafie
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Parti Warisan remains strong despite the recent departure of several former leaders, its president Datuk Seri Mohd Shafie Apdal said. Shafie said those who left the party were merely a handful of former division chiefs and former office-bearers. The former chief minister was referring to several leaders, including former Warisan Supreme Council member and Kota Belud Warisan vice-chief Taib Isai, who recently joined Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor's Parti Gagasan Rakyat Sabah.

items.title: Pensioner remanded in Johor after granddaughter alleges repeated sexual abuse
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A 67-year-old pensioner has been remanded for six days after his teenage granddaughter alleged repeated sexual abuse over the past year, police said. Magistrate Arun Noval Dass granted the remand order to allow police to complete investigations under Section 376(3) of the Penal Code. Police received a report two days ago after the victim confided in her mother.

items.title: Two babies found as police, Perhilitan arrest eight Myanmar nationals in Kelantan wildlife raid
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 10 — Eight Myanmar nationals, including two women, were arrested in a joint police–Perhilitan operation against illegal hunting and wildlife offences at a 24-hectare durian plantation in Kampung Mia, Kuala Krai, today. Kuala Krai district police chief Supt Mazlan Mamat said that acting on a public tip-off, police and the Wildlife and National Parks Department jointly conducted Op Bersepadu/Op Khazanah from midnight until 6am. “Inspections also uncovered six suspected wildlife claws, 12 suspected wildlife fangs, seven suspected wildlife bones, six suspected wildlife body parts, as well as a container filled with meat believed to be from wildlife,” he said in a statement.

items.title: Rotary Club Bangsar taps media partnerships to amplify aid for B40, Orang Asli communities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Rotary Club Bangsar is seeking closer collaboration with media organisations to raise public awareness of its humanitarian and community outreach efforts. Ravinder said Rotary Club Bangsar regularly organises charitable and outreach programmes, including medical aid and welfare initiatives for Orang Asli communities and the B40 group. At the Editorial Floor, the delegation was received by Media Prima News and Current Affairs deputy managing editor and New Straits Times group editor Farrah Naz Abd Karim, NST deputy group editor Sharanjit Singh and Berita Harian deputy group editor M. Thillinadan.

items.title: Rosmah faces bankruptcy risk if RM67.5mil judgment remains unpaid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Lebanese jeweller Global Royalty Trading SAL Ltd will pursue all legal avenues to recover the RM67.5 million awarded by the High Court against Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor, including bankruptcy proceedings as a last resort. Senior lawyer Datuk David Gurupatham, who represented the company, said his client intended to fully enforce the judgment delivered today. However, David said bankruptcy proceedings would not be the company's immediate course of action if Rosmah fails to satisfy the judgment within the one-month period ordered by the court.

items.title: OGX unit bags RM96mil data centre supply contract
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: OGX Group Bhd has secured a RM96.6 million contract to supply critical infrastructure products for a data centre project. The internet technology (IT) infrastructure solutions specialist said its subsidiary, OGX Networks Sdn Bhd has received a letter of award (LOA) for the supply of fibre optic cables, patch panels and related materials for data centre applications. Executive director and managing director Tan Suan Loong said this reflects the confidence placed in OGX Group as a reliable partner in supplying data centre products.

items.title: Bank staff prevent over RM139mil in fraud attempts last year
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 68 bank employees have helped prevent more than RM139 million in fraudulent transactions in 2025 by detecting suspicious activities and intervening in potential scam cases. The employees were recognised at the #JanganKenaScam Bank Heroes Recognition 2026, an awareness programme introduced in 2023 by the Association of Banks in Malaysia (ABM) and the Association of Islamic Banking and Financial Institutions Malaysia (AIBIM). The national taskforce to combat fraud chairman Syed Ahmad Taufik Albar said the total value of the prevented fraudulent transactions rose to RM1.19 billion in 2025.

items.title: Military court jails Indonesian soldiers over acid attack on activist
items.summary: JAKARTA: A military court in Indonesia on Wednesday sentenced four soldiers to up to three years in prison for an acid attack on an activist critical of perceived militarisation of the government. The four, all members of the military's Strategic Intelligence Agency (BAIS), were found guilty of premeditated assault for the attack in March that left Andrie Yunus, 27, blind in one eye. Soldiers Edi Sudarko, 45; Budi Hariyanto Widhi Cahyono, 43; Nandala Dwi Prasetia, 40; and Sami Lakka, 41, engaged in "arrogant conduct", presiding judge Fredy Ferdian Isnartanto said.

items.title: Kelantan ranks second in fuel smuggling cases nationwide under Ops Tiris 4.0
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Kelantan recorded the second-highest number of fuel smuggling cases nationwide under Ops Tiris 4.0 (Integrated), with 57 cases detected between March 16 and June 3 this year. Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry enforcement director-general Datuk Azman Adam said only Sabah recorded a higher number of cases, with 58, while Kedah ranked third with 15 cases. Azman said attempts to smuggle controlled goods across Malaysia's borders were being met with intensified enforcement following the launch of Ops Tiris 4.0 in March.

items.title: Trump looms large over Brazil's presidential election
items.summary: US President Donald Trump's sometimes bombastic stance towards Brazil has placed him at the centre of debate ahead of the country's tightly fought presidential elections in October. The US leader's influence could be less clear-cut this time around, however. While celebrating his "excellent chemistry" with leftist President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva, who is seeking reelection, Trump has also called candidate Flavio Bolsonaro, the son of former far-right president Jair Bolsonaro, a "smart young man who loves his country."

items.title: Annuar Musa appointed Johor Pas election director for state polls
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Pas central committee member Tan Sri Annuar Musa has been appointed Johor Pas election director for the 16th state election. "Johor Pas welcomes the appointment of Tan Sri Annuar Musa as election director and is confident his experience and leadership will strengthen preparations and coordination of the party machinery for the Johor election. Meanwhile, Mohd Firdaus said Johor Pas fully supported the party leadership's decision to end political cooperation with Bersatu.

items.title: Mahfuz: Pas-Bersatu 'mock divorce' insults voters
items.summary: ALOR SETAR: Amanah described the ongoing political rift between Pas and Bersatu within PN as "political theatre" and a "mock divorce" driven by power struggles rather than principles. Amanah vice-president Datuk Mahfuz Omar said the latest move by the Pas leadership to end political cooperation with Bersatu while retaining its leadership position within PN was contradictory and an insult to the voters' intelligence. Mahfuz said the internal developments showed that political survival was being prioritised over principle.

items.title: LHDN scam alerts: What to do if you receive suspicious tax demands
items.summary: Check directly with LHDN to verify if it is a scam or not: If you are in Malaysia, call LHDN (03-8911 1000); If you are overseas, call LHDN (603-8911 1000); Use the Live Chat function; Use the online Customer Feedback Form; Visit the nearest LHDN office. Check LHDN’s website for the official phone numbers. LHDN’s official email addresses end with @hasil.gov.my.

items.title: Sabah youth among first to benefit from Perkeso's 24-hour protection
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A young man from Sabah who suffered a serious spinal injury after falling down a staircase at home has become among the first beneficiaries of the Non-Work-Related Accident Scheme, or Lindung 24/7 protection scheme, which came into effect on June 1. In a statement today, the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) said Arnold Nathanael Lister, 24, qualified for coverage as an active contributor despite the accident occurring outside working hours. Perkeso said the case highlighted the role of the scheme in protecting workers injured outside the scope of their employment.

items.title: Johor Customs foils RM8.5mil transnational cigarette smuggling bid at Tanjung Pelepas port
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor Customs enforcement officers foiled a transnational cigarette smuggling attempt from Vietnam to the United Kingdom, seizing more than RM8.5 million worth of illicit cigarettes at the Tanjung Pelepas Port last month. The 9.32 million sticks of cigarettes, valued at RM2.14 million with unpaid duties and taxes estimated at RM6.36 million, were discovered during a warehouse inspection within the port free zone on May 18. Johor Customs director Aminul Izmeer Mohd Sohaimi said the 12.19-metre container was inspected at the warehouse at 2.30 pm.

items.title: Anwar: Japanese support vital as Malaysia builds AI, semiconductor and quantum capabilities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — The Japanese government and industry support is critical to helping Malaysia build its capabilities in advanced technologies, particularly semiconductors, artificial intelligence (AI), quantum computing and advanced packaging, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said the country is positioning itself to move beyond traditional growth sectors by embracing digitalisation, AI and other emerging technologies, while strengthening its role as a regional semiconductor hub. Anwar said Malaysia is also focusing on developing capabilities in quantum technologies, noting that the country is learning from international partners that are further ahead in the field.

items.title: Foreign-run businesses among 22 targeted in DBKL enforcement operation at Jalan Yew
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — Sixteen foreign nationals were detained, and goods belonging to 22 unlicensed traders were seized during an integrated enforcement operation around Pudu Market in Jalan Yew here yesterday. In a Facebook post, Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) said the operation, which began at 6am, was carried out in collaboration with the Malaysian Immigration Department (JIM), targeting the sale of fresh produce, vegetables and fruits by foreign nationals operating without authorisation and in breach of their work permit conditions. “Enforcement action was taken against 22 unlicensed traders, comprising 13 foreign nationals and nine Malaysians.

items.title: Engineering consultants push for 40–50pc hike as fees stagnate since 1983
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 (Bernama) — Engineering consultancy fees in Malaysia have increased by less than 10 per cent since 1983 despite a significant rise in project complexity and workload, according to The Institution of Engineers Malaysia (IEM). A survey titled “Engineering Impact Survey — Scale of Fees for Engineering Consultancy Firms”, conducted from May 15 to 24, 2026, gathered perspectives from 598 professionals, IEM said in a recent statement. Its members also recommended raising consultancy fees by 40 to 50 per cent to reflect current compliance requirements, operational costs and professional liabilities.

items.title: MPV collides with ambulance transporting crash victims to Besul Hospital, triggering chain collision
items.summary: JERTIH, June 10 — A Ministry of Health ambulance carrying two patients from an earlier motorcycle crash was involved in a triple-vehicle collision in Kampung Cherang Meliling, off Jalan Jertih–Pasir Akar, yesterday evening. District police chief Supt Mohd Rozaime Ab Rahman said the accident occurred at 5.00pm as the ambulance was en route to Besut Hospital. All individuals involved, including the ambulance driver and MPV driver, escaped injury, although the front of the ambulance was damaged.

items.title: Received an LHDN email demanding action in 72 hours? Here’s how to spot if it’s a scam
items.summary: This screengrab shows part of the first tax scam email received by a Malay Mail reader. What the tax scam emails looked like The first scam email came with the official-sounding title “Notis Tuntutan Cukai & Penalti (Akta 53)”, giving the impression that it was a notice from LHDN. This screengrab shows another section of the first tax scam email.

items.title: Api-Api rep flags apparent discrepancies in Kota Kinabalu BAS.MY rollout, demands Sabah govt to explain fully
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 10 — Api-Api Assemblyman Loi Kok Liang backed calls for the authorities to publicly disclose full details of the BAS.MY Kota Kinabalu bus service, saying Sabahans deserve clear answers on whether they are receiving the quality of public transport they were promised. “The Sabah Government, the Ministry of Transport, the Land Public Transport Agency and the appointed operator should publish the fleet composition, procurement specifications, accessibility provisions and a breakdown of how programme funding is being utilised under the Stage Bus Service Transformation programme,” he said. Loi: Sabahans deserve clear answers “The questions raised by Seen are legitimate and deserve clear answers.

items.title: Japan rolls out guard of honour for Anwar in first official PM trip to Tokyo
items.summary: TOKYO, June 10 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim was today accorded an official welcoming ceremony at the Japanese Prime Minister’s Office in Tokyo, marking his first official visit to Japan since taking office. Anwar was then introduced to Japanese Cabinet ministers and senior government officials, after which he introduced the Malaysian delegation to Takaichi. This visit is Anwar’s first official trip to Japan since becoming Malaysia’s 10th Prime Minister in November 2022, though he previously made two working visits in 2023 and 2024.

items.title: Anwar urges firm stand against discriminatory tariffs, calls for stronger free trade voice
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — Sovereign countries should not be timid in defending free trade and opposing punitive tariffs or discriminatory policies, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar said as a sovereign interdependent country, Malaysia took its position on tariffs very clearly, and has been appealing to countries to be more affirmative and more pronounced in their views on free trade and multilateralism. Do we support free trade?

items.title: Sarawak TVET team clinches runner-up spot at Taiwan robotics championship
items.summary: KUCHING, June 10 — Matang Vocational College has brought pride to Sarawak and Malaysia after its robotics team secured a runner-up finish at an international robotics competition held in Taiwan recently. The team won the Finalist Alliance Award in the League of Mecha-Clash (LOM) category at the STVE x MARC Open Championship 2026, held at the National Taiwan Science Education Centre in Taipei from May 30 to 31. The championship attracted top robotics teams from various countries, providing a platform for participants to demonsrate their expertise in robot design, programming, engineering and strategic problem-solving.

items.title: ‘Could not be proven’: NFA for Albert Tei’s complaint against ex-MACC chief Azam Baki, says AGC
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — The Attorney General’s Chambers (AGC) has decided to classify the investigation paper involving businessman Tei Jiann Cheing, better known as Albert Tei, and his complaint against Tan Sri Azam Baki as No Further Action (NFA). In a media statement today, the AGC said it had reviewed the investigation paper submitted by the Royal Malaysia Police over Tei’s complaint against Azam for an alleged offence under Section 506 of the Penal Code. Section 506 of the Penal Code covers criminal intimidation.

items.title: At Nikkei Forum, Anwar calls for leadership with clear sense of direction
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — Sovereign countries should not be timid in defending free trade and opposing punitive tariffs or discriminatory policies, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar said as a sovereign interdependent country, Malaysia took its position on tariffs very clearly, and has been appealing to countries to be more affirmative and more pronounced in their views on free trade and multilateralism. Do we support free trade?

items.title: Former MCA president says PAS could win Putrajaya ‘by default’ if Chinese voters stay home
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — Former MCA president Tan Sri Dr Chua Soi Lek has reportedly said that PAS could win federal power “by default” in the next general election if Chinese voters avoid the polls or intentionally cast spoilt votes. According to Free Malaysia Today (FMT) , Chua said the possibility was already being “debated quietly” among Chinese voters, with some Pakatan Harapan (PH) leaders believing that PAS could form the federal government if voters do not back the coalition. Chua reportedly said PAS would find it hard to gain ground in Sabah and Sarawak, which together have 56 parliamentary seats, while DAP is still expected to hold an advantage in 32 Chinese-majority seats.

items.title: High Court orders Rosmah to pay Lebanese owner RM67.4m in missing jewellery suit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — The High Court today ordered Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor to pay RM67.4 million — the estimated value of 43 jewellery pieces delivered to her in February 2018 — to their Lebanese owner. High Court judge Quay Chew Soon granted the suit filed by Global Royalty Trading SAL against her, which sought to recover the 43 jewellery items or their claimed value of US$14.6 million after the items were seized during a police raid at Pavilion Residences in 2018. Quay said the court found Rosmah liable for the missing jewellery pieces.

items.title: As Modi sets tenure record, Anwar praises leader and Malaysia-India friendship
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has congratulated Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi on his historic milestone of becoming the country’s longest-serving elected prime minister. “Heartiest congratulations to PM Modi on this historic milestone of becoming India’s longest-serving elected prime minister,” he said. Modi has been serving as Prime Minister since May 2014.

items.title: Annuar Musa: PAS to meet with Perikatan components, sans Bersatu, on coalition direction this week
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 10 — PAS will discuss Perikatan Nasional’s (PN) position with Gerakan and the Malaysian Indian People’s Party (MIPP) this week, after ending its political cooperation with Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu). PAS central committee member and PN information chief Tan Sri Annuar Musa said the decision was “nothing personal”, adding that it only involved political cooperation between PAS and Bersatu, and did not involve PN as a coalition. Annuar said PAS remained committed to multiracial political cooperation and appreciated the positive attitude shown by Gerakan and MIPP within PN.

items.title: From plane to six-hour Grab ride: Passenger spends RM700 after AirBorneo axes Tawau–Sandakan flight
items.summary: SIBU, June 10 — Datuk Betty Wong had to spend more than RM700 on Grab fares and endure a gruelling nearly six-hour journey to attend a training seminar in Sandakan on June 7 after her AirBorneo flight from Tawau to Sandakan was cancelled. When contacted by The Borneo Post on Monday, the Lions Club past district governor (PDG) said AirBorneo flight MY3062 from Tawau to Sandakan on June 7 was initially rescheduled from 10.15am that day to 12.55pm on June 8. “We actually had a seminar scheduled for 1pm on June 7 in Sandakan.

items.title: Miri’s main water artery springs leak, water supply disruption to affect over 70,000 consumers
items.summary: MIRI, June 10 — Sarawak Water Sdn Bhd (Sarawak Water) has confirmed that the 1,100mm main pipeline at Mile 7 Miri-Bintulu Road is the source of a major leak here. The list of areas in Miri, Sarawak affected by the water supply interruption. For more information, contact Sarawak Water on 085-426626.

items.title: Johor PAS commissioner says ties with Bersatu still friendly despite split
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 10 — Johor PAS will maintain friendly ties with Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) at the state level despite having to officially sever ties, said Johor PAS Commissioner Datuk Mahfodz Mohamed. “The Johor Bersatu leadership had in fact contacted me regarding the decision (on Monday night). “Despite that, I assured him Johor PAS will continue our friendship with the state Bersatu,” he added Mahfodz, who is Johor PN deputy chairman, was responding to the recent announcement that PAS had cut ties with Bersatu, effectively ending its six-year political cooperation.

items.title: Selangor to make universal design a development requirement under new PWD blueprint
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 10 — The Selangor govt today launched the Selangor Persons with Disabilities Policy and Action Plan 2026-2030 to strengthen inclusion and support the holistic development of the state’s approximately 150,000 registered persons with disabilities (PWDs). State Women Development and Social Welfare Committee chairman Anfaal Saari said the comprehensive policy marked a significant shift from a passive welfare-based approach towards one centred on rights, active participation and empowerment. On accessibility, Anfaal acknowledged that making older developments fully PWD-friendly could be challenging, but said improvements could begin with new developments.
